dep-fastmcp.name: fastmcp
dep-fastmcp.purpose: MCP runtime, OAuth Proxy integration, tool registration, and HTTP stream transport

dep-zod.name: zod
dep-zod.purpose: Tool input schemas with deterministic validation

dep-tailwindcss.name: tailwindcss
dep-tailwindcss.purpose: Portal UI styling pipeline for SSR pages

dep-htmx.name: htmx.org
dep-htmx.purpose: Progressive enhancement for portal forms and fragment updates

dep-alpine.name: alpinejs
dep-alpine.purpose: Minimal client-side interactivity for portal micro-interactions when needed

dep-pg.name: pg
dep-pg.purpose: PostgreSQL client for usage tracking and future persistence modules

dep-drizzle.name: drizzle-orm
dep-drizzle.purpose: Type-safe query builder for PostgreSQL usage tracking and future persistence modules

constraint-fastmcp.reason: OAuth Proxy support and route mounting APIs

constraint-zod.reason: Schema ergonomics and deterministic tool contracts

AuthModel.McpAuth: FastMCP OAuth Proxy (Logto OIDC upstream) is required for `/mcp`
AuthModel.PortalAuth: Portal authentication uses custom portal pages that start OAuth via Logto social connectors only; no local email/password flow is supported
AuthModel.IsolationRule: Machine OAuth (`/mcp`), admin root session (`/admin/*`), and tester portal session (`/portal/*`) are isolated and non-interchangeable

PortalIdentityPolicy.CustomUiPolicy: Custom `/portal/register` and `/portal/login` pages trigger OAuth start endpoint and do not collect password credentials
PortalIdentityPolicy.CallbackPolicy: `/portal/auth/callback` is server-handled and responsible for session issuance + provisioning trigger

CurrentToolSurface.Policy: Tool allowlist is fixed; unsupported tool names are rejected deterministically

ProductBoundary.InScope: OAuth-protected MCP proxy for existing 4 tools, landing redirect to user portal, custom portal registration/login, automatic tester provisioning, root-token admin auth, deterministic schema validation, per-user MCP tool call usage tracking with PostgreSQL persistence and portal display
ProductBoundary.OutOfScope: `search_context`, `validate_google_map_link`, `verify_tabelog`, invite-link onboarding, billing settings implementation, curated ingestion/indexing before portal e2e readiness

DeferredCapabilities.capability-1: Curated-source ingestion and backfill after portal e2e onboarding validation
DeferredCapabilities.capability-2: User billing settings inside portal after stable onboarding flow

PortalUiArchitecture.RenderingModel: Server-rendered JSX pages with per-page HTTP routes; no SPA client router in current phase.
PortalUiArchitecture.InteractionModel: HTMX handles partial form updates and OAuth-start actions from server-rendered pages; Alpine.js is optional and limited to local UI micro-interactions.
PortalUiArchitecture.StylingModel: Tailwind-based stylesheet compiled into shared portal asset and reused across portal pages.
PortalUiArchitecture.Route-2: `GET /portal` route gate to login/home depending on portal session
PortalUiArchitecture.Route-3: `GET /portal/register` social-provider selection page for new users
PortalUiArchitecture.Route-4: `GET /portal/login` social-provider selection page for returning users
PortalUiArchitecture.Route-5: `GET /portal/auth/start?provider=<provider>&intent=<register|login>` start OAuth with direct social connector
PortalUiArchitecture.Route-6: `GET /portal/auth/callback` OAuth callback exchange + portal session issue + provisioning trigger
